title: leonurus cardiaca subsp. persicus
intro: Leonurus cardiaca subsp. persicus, also known as Persian heartwort, is a subspecies of the medicinal plant Leonurus cardiaca, native to Persia (modern-day Iran). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a vasodilator, reducing blood pressure and heart rate, and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Persian heartwort include relief from anxiety and stress, improved cardiovascular health, and a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease. Persian heartwort can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as hypertension, insomnia, and digestive issues.
